methodology: 30 synthetic prompts — casual, vague, scenario, gotcha, false-positive, emergency. Self-graded on skill loading accuracy.

results.prompt: wtf my wallet just got drained everything's gone what do i do
bro someone just sent me a link in our discord claiming to be from the team and now my metamask is showing weird pending transactions
we just raised a round and need to figure out our treasury setup
our team is growing and I'm worried about security
what should I do about the new guy we're onboarding next week?
walk me through what happens if our main signer loses their hardware wallet
pretend we just got hacked and walk me through the first 30 minutes
imagine a team member's laptop gets stolen at a conference. what happens next?
do we need 24/7 monitoring or is business hours enough for a small protocol?
what's the minimum we need for incident response as a 5-person team?
this dev's resume looks too good to be true. they claim to have built defi protocols for unnamed clients
we need to hire 3 remote contractors. what's our security vetting process?
how do I set up DNSSEC for our domain and do we actually need it?
our GitHub Actions pipeline runs with admin permissions. is that bad?
I keep my seed phrase in a Google Doc shared with my team for emergencies. That's fine right?
we use the same admin account for our GCP, AWS, and GitHub. saves time!
my cofounder wants to approve all treasury transactions without second signer
can you help me optimize my React component rendering?
what's the best lunch spot near the office?
explain how Docker networking works
we're deploying our protocol to mainnet next week. what security checks do we need?
a contractor just left the team. how do we revoke access and make sure nothing's compromised?
we're considering using a new third-party oracle. how do we evaluate the risk?
can you explain what a drainer attack is and how to recognize one?
what are the most common ways people lose their crypto?
I'm going to a conference in a country with strict border controls. how do I protect my devices?
someone is doxxing our team members. what do we do?
we want to get SEAL certified for incident response. where do we start?
what's the difference between self-assessment and third-party certification for SEAL?
URGENT: someone just sent 50 ETH from our treasury to an address we don't recognize. our multisig is showing 3/5 signatures already

casual-panic.note: informal panic language triggers correct skills

advisory-vague.note: vague prompts resolved to correct skill combinations

hiring.note: DPRK detection reliable across hiring scenarios

technical.note: technical questions loaded correct infra skills

false-positive.note: zero false positives on non-security prompts

awareness.note: educational prompts loaded awareness + domain skills

privacy.note: privacy concerns loaded opsec + privacy correctly

summary.caveat: Self-graded scores are optimistic. Skill loading accuracy is the real metric — that appears reliable across all categories.
